examples.sentence: 愛がゆえの悲しみ。
examples.translation: Sadness due to love.
examples.translationEn: Sadness due to love.
examples.romaji: Ai ga yue no kanashimi.

examples.sentence: 健康がゆえの幸せ。
examples.translation: Happiness due to good health.
examples.translationEn: Happiness due to good health.
examples.romaji: Kenkou ga yue no shiawase.

examples.sentence: 悪行がゆえの罰。
examples.translation: Punishment due to wrongdoing.
examples.translationEn: Punishment due to wrongdoing.
examples.romaji: Akugyou ga yue no batsu.

examples.sentence: 努力がゆえの成功。
examples.translation: Success due to effort.
examples.translationEn: Success due to effort.
examples.romaji: Doryoku ga yue no seikou.

examples.grammarPoint: ～から Noun に 至る まで
examples.sentence: このバンドの曲は、青年から老人に至るまで、人々に愛されています。
examples.translation: The songs of this band are loved by people ranging from the young to the elderly.
examples.translationEn: The songs of this band are loved by people ranging from the young to the elderly.
examples.romaji: Kono bando no kyoku wa, seinen kara roujin ni itaru made, hitobito ni aisarete imasu.

examples.grammarPoint: ～から Noun に 至る まで
examples.sentence: 小さい物件から大きな建物に至るまで、全てのプロパティの管理を我々が行います。
examples.translation: We handle all property management, ranging from small units to large buildings.
examples.translationEn: We handle all property management, ranging from small units to large buildings.
examples.romaji: Chiisai bukken kara ooki na tatemono ni itaru made, subete no property no kanri o wareware ga okonaimasu.

examples.grammarPoint: ～から Noun に 至る まで
examples.sentence: 朝のニュース放送から夜のドラマに至るまで、テレビは毎日見ています。
examples.translation: I watch TV every day, from the morning news broadcasts to the night dramas.
examples.translationEn: I watch TV every day, from the morning news broadcasts to the night dramas.
examples.romaji: Asa no nyuusu housou kara yoru no dorama ni itaru made, terebi wa mainichi mite imasu.

examples.grammarPoint: ～から Noun に 至る まで
examples.sentence: この地域は、春から冬に至るまで旅行者で賑わいます。
examples.translation: This area is bustling with tourists from spring to winter.
examples.translationEn: This area is bustling with tourists from spring to winter.
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki wa, haru kara fuyu ni itaru made ryokousha de nigiwaimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は鳥のごとく飛んでいるように見えた。
examples.translation: He looked as though he was flying like a bird.
examples.translationEn: He looked as though he was flying like a bird.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tori no gotoku tonde iru you ni mieta.

examples.sentence: 彼は死んだごとく眠っていた。
examples.translation: He was sleeping as though he were dead.
examples.translationEn: He was sleeping as though he were dead.
examples.romaji: Kare wa shinda gotoku nemutte ita.

examples.sentence: 彼女はアイドルのごとく崇められている。
examples.translation: She is revered as if she were an idol.
examples.translationEn: She is revered as if she were an idol.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa aidoru no gotoku agamerarete iru.

examples.sentence: 彼は風のごとく走った。
examples.translation: He ran like the wind.
examples.translationEn: He ran like the wind.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kaze no gotoku hashitta.

examples.sentence: 彼は裁判に勝つために全資産を使うこととて、弁護士に頼んだ。
examples.translation: Since he's using all his assets to win the trial, he hired a lawyer.
examples.translationEn: Since he's using all his assets to win the trial, he hired a lawyer.
examples.romaji: Kare wa saiban ni katsu tame ni zenshisan o tsukau koto tote, bengoshi ni tanonda.

examples.sentence: 明日は大切な試験があることとて、今夜は早く寝ます。
examples.translation: Since I have an important exam tomorrow, I’m going to bed early tonight.
examples.translationEn: Since I have an important exam tomorrow, I’m going to bed early tonight.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa taisetsu na shiken ga aru koto tote, konya wa hayaku nemasu.

examples.sentence: このプロジェクトの成功は会社の未来に影響することとて、全力を尽くします。
examples.translation: Because the success of this project will affect the company's future, I will do my best.
examples.translationEn: Because the success of this project will affect the company's future, I will do my best.
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto no seikou wa kaisha no mirai ni eikyou suru koto tote, zenryoku o tsukushimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が有名人であることとて、プライバシーが保護されるべきだ。
examples.translation: Since he is a celebrity, his privacy should be protected.
examples.translationEn: Since he is a celebrity, his privacy should be protected.
examples.romaji: Kare ga yuumeijin de aru koto tote, puraibashii ga hogo sareru beki da.

examples.sentence: 彼は事故を起こさずにすんだ。
examples.translation: He managed to avoid having an accident.
examples.translationEn: He managed to avoid having an accident.
examples.romaji: Kare wa jiko o okosazu ni sunda.

examples.sentence: 私は手術せずにすんだ。
examples.translation: I was able to do without surgery.
examples.translationEn: I was able to do without surgery.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa shujutsu sezu ni sunda.

examples.sentence: 雨が降らずにすんだ。
examples.translation: We managed to avoid the rain.
examples.translationEn: We managed to avoid the rain.
examples.romaji: Ame ga furazu ni sunda.

examples.sentence: 罰金を払わずにすんだ。
examples.translation: I managed to avoid paying a fine.
examples.translationEn: I managed to avoid paying a fine.
examples.romaji: Bakkin o harawazu ni sunda.

examples.sentence: 試験が難しいだろうとなかろうと、全力で頑張ります。
examples.translation: Regardless of whether the exam is difficult or not, I will do my best.
examples.translationEn: Regardless of whether the exam is difficult or not, I will do my best.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga muzukashii darou to nakarou to, zenryoku de ganbarimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が来るだろうとなかろうと、私たちは始めます。
examples.translation: Whether he comes or not, we will start.
examples.translationEn: Whether he comes or not, we will start.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru darou to nakarou to, watashitachi wa hajimemasu.

examples.sentence: この仕事が面白いだろうとなかろうと、私は続けます。
examples.translation: Whether this job is interesting or not, I will continue.
examples.translationEn: Whether this job is interesting or not, I will continue.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ga omoshiroi darou to nakarou to, watashi wa tsuzukemasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が賛成するだろうとなかろうと、私たちは進行します。
examples.translation: Whether he agrees or not, we will proceed.
examples.translationEn: Whether he agrees or not, we will proceed.
examples.romaji: Kare ga sansei suru darou to nakarou to, watashitachi wa shinkou shimasu.

examples.sentence: 明日は早く起きるつもりです。
examples.translation: I plan to wake up early tomorrow.
examples.translationEn: I plan to wake up early tomorrow.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa hayaku okiru tsumori desu.

examples.sentence: 私は日本語を勉強するつもりです。
examples.translation: I intend to study Japanese.
examples.translationEn: I intend to study Japanese.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa nihongo o benkyou suru tsumori desu.

examples.sentence: 彼は留学するつもりだと言った。
examples.translation: He said he plans to study abroad.
examples.translationEn: He said he plans to study abroad.
examples.romaji: Kare wa ryuugaku suru tsumori da to itta.

examples.sentence: 私は来週末に家族と旅行するつもりだ。
examples.translation: I plan to travel with my family next weekend.
examples.translationEn: I plan to travel with my family next weekend.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa raishuumatsu ni kazoku to ryokou suru tsumori da.

examples.sentence: 私は日本に住むつもりで日本語を勉強しています。
examples.translation: I am studying Japanese with the intention of living in Japan.
examples.translationEn: I am studying Japanese with the intention of living in Japan.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa Nihon ni sumu tsumori de Nihongo o benkyou shiteimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は全てを失うつもりでギャンブルをしました。
examples.translation: He gambled with the intention of risking everything.
examples.translationEn: He gambled with the intention of risking everything.
examples.romaji: Kare wa subete o ushinau tsumori de gyanburu o shimashita.

examples.sentence: 明日のテストに合格するつもりで一生懸命勉強します。
examples.translation: I will study hard with the intention of passing tomorrow’s test.
examples.translationEn: I will study hard with the intention of passing tomorrow’s test.
examples.romaji: Ashita no tesuto ni goukaku suru tsumori de isshoukenmei benkyou shimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は早く起きるつもりで早く寝ました。
examples.translation: He went to bed early with the intention of waking up early.
examples.translationEn: He went to bed early with the intention of waking up early.
examples.romaji: Kare wa hayaku okiru tsumori de hayaku nemashita.

examples.sentence: ただ謝るではすまない。何か行動を取らなければならない。
examples.translation: Just apologizing will not suffice. Some action needs to be taken.
examples.translationEn: Just apologizing will not suffice. Some action needs to be taken.
examples.romaji: Tada ayamaru dewa sumanai. Nanika koudou o toranakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: この問題は議論だけではすまない。具体的な計画が必要だ。
examples.translation: This problem won't be solved with just discussions. A concrete plan is needed.
examples.translationEn: This problem won't be solved with just discussions. A concrete plan is needed.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa giron dake dewa sumanai. Gutaiteki na keikaku ga hitsuyou da.

examples.sentence: 彼の失敗は注意だけではすまない。彼はその責任を取るべきだ。
examples.translation: His failure won't be resolved with just a warning. He should take responsibility.
examples.translationEn: His failure won't be resolved with just a warning. He should take responsibility.
examples.romaji: Kare no shippai wa chuui dake dewa sumanai. Kare wa sono sekinin o toru beki da.

examples.sentence: この失敗は金だけではすまない。信頼を取り戻すため、真心で謝罪するべきだ。
examples.translation: This mistake cannot be settled with money alone. To regain trust, an earnest apology is needed.
examples.translationEn: This mistake cannot be settled with money alone. To regain trust, an earnest apology is needed.
examples.romaji: Kono shippai wa kane dake dewa sumanai. Shinrai o torimodosu tame, magokoro de shazai suru beki da.

examples.sentence: 春休みとあって、公園は子供たちでいっぱいだ。
examples.translation: Because it's spring break, the park is full of children.
examples.translationEn: Because it's spring break, the park is full of children.
examples.romaji: Haruyasumi to atte, kouen wa kodomo-tachi de ippai da.

examples.sentence: 試験前とあって、図書館は学生で混んでいる。
examples.translation: With exams coming up, the library is crowded with students.
examples.translationEn: With exams coming up, the library is crowded with students.
examples.romaji: Shiken mae to atte, toshokan wa gakusei de konde iru.

examples.sentence: 開店日とあって、その店にはたくさんの人が並んでいた。
examples.translation: Because it was the store's opening day, a lot of people lined up.
examples.translationEn: Because it was the store's opening day, a lot of people lined up.
examples.romaji: Kaitenbi to atte, sono mise ni wa takusan no hito ga narande ita.

examples.sentence: 彼の誕生日とあって、友人たちはサプライズパーティーを計画した。
examples.translation: Because it was his birthday, his friends planned a surprise party.
examples.translationEn: Because it was his birthday, his friends planned a surprise party.
examples.romaji: Kare no tanjoubi to atte, yuujin-tachi wa sapuraizu paatii o keikaku shita.
